request.description:
Mobile home dashboard with submission overview + project sections.

Filters:
- `invited_status`: `all` | `planned` | `inprogress` | `in_progress` | `completed`
- `invited_search`: text
- `invited_page`: integer >= 1
- `invited_limit`: integer 1..100
- `area_status`: `all` | `planned` | `inprogress` | `in_progress` | `completed`
- `area_search`: text
- `area_limit`: integer 1..100

Filter URL examples:
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/home?invited_status=planned&invited_page=1&invited_limit=10`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/home?invited_status=in_progress&invited_search=market`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/home?area_status=completed&area_search=school&area_limit=5`

request.description:
Get dynamic reporting schema/options for the mobile submission UI.

Filters:
- `project_id` (optional): returns project defaults and enforces municipality scope for reporter/focal point.

Important response sections:
- `flow.steps`: question-by-question reporting schema by project status.
- `submission_contract.field_aliases.activities_started`: alias for the completed-status question.
- `submission_contract.media_upload_flow`: exact draft -> presign -> upload binary -> complete -> attach flow for assets.

Completed-status question key:
- Preferred mobile field: `activity_started` with `Yes` / `No`
- Canonical stored field: `activities_started`
- Also accepted: `activities_workshops_or_training_started`

Filter URL examples:
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/reporting/options`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/reporting/options?project_id={{project_id}}`

request.description:
List submissions focused on submitted tab.

Filters:
- `tab`: `submitted` | `drafts` | `all` (default: `submitted`)
- `status`: `draft` | `queued` | `submitted` | `under_review` | `approved` | `rework_requested` | `rejected` | `rework` (alias to `rework_requested`)
- `search`: free text over title/details/id/project names
- `page`: integer >= 1
- `per_page`: integer 1..100
- `limit`: alias for `per_page` (backward compatibility)

Filter URL examples:
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/submissions?tab=all&status=approved&page=1&per_page=20`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/submissions?tab=submitted&status=rejected&page=1&per_page=20`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/submissions?tab=submitted&status=rework&page=1&per_page=20`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/submissions?tab=drafts&page=1&per_page=10`
- `{{base_url}}/mobile/submissions?tab=all&search=reinforcement&page=2&per_page=10`

response.name: 200 Submitted List

originalRequest.description:
List submissions focused on submitted tab.

Filters:

request.description:
Create or idempotently reuse a mobile submission.

Collection example uses `form-data`.
- Upload one or more attachments using repeated `assets[]` file fields.
- If you are attaching existing uploaded assets by ID instead of files, those asset IDs still require an existing `submission_id`.

Notes:
- `mode` can be `draft` or `submit`.
- If `mode` is omitted on create, backend defaults to `draft`.
- `client_uuid` enables idempotent create/retry behavior.
- Submit-only required fields are enforced only when mode resolves to `submit`.
- Uploaded files are stored locally under `storage/app/public/mobile/assets/{submission_id}/` and returned as `/storage/mobile/assets/{submission_id}/...` URLs.

Presigned upload note:
- `POST /mobile/media/presign-upload` is only available for S3-compatible storage and returns `422` on local public storage.

request.description:
Submit/update an in-progress report (`project_status=in_progress`) using `form-data` sent as `POST` with `_method=PUT`.

Required on submit for in-progress:
- `project_status`, `confirm_accuracy`, `actual_beneficiaries`, `location_label` (or lat/lng)
- `progress_impression`, `physical_progress`, `approximate_completion_percentage`, `additional_observations`

Attachments:
- Upload one or more files using repeated `assets[]` file fields.
- If you are attaching existing uploaded assets instead of files, send asset ids as text fields.

Transport note:
- Keep the HTTP method as `POST` in Postman and send `_method=PUT`. PHP does not parse uploaded files on raw multipart PUT requests.

originalRequest.description:
Submit/update a completed report (`project_status=completed`) using `form-data` sent as `POST` with `_method=PUT`.

Completed-specific required on submit:
- `is_project_being_used`, `is_used_as_intended`, `functional_status`, `negative_environmental_impact`
- `user_categories` when `is_project_being_used=true`

Completed-status question field:
- Preferred mobile key: `activity_started` with `Yes` / `No`
- Canonical stored key: `activities_started`
- Also accepted: `activities_workshops_or_training_started`

Attachments:
- Upload one or more files using repeated `assets[]` file fields.
- If you are attaching existing uploaded assets instead of files, send asset ids as text fields.

Transport note:
- Keep the HTTP method as `POST` in Postman and send `_method=PUT`. PHP does not parse uploaded files on raw multipart PUT requests.

request.description:
Create/reuse a presigned upload for image/video evidence on S3-compatible storage only.

Use this only after a submission already exists, because `submission_id` is required.

Body options:
- `submission_id`: existing draft/submission id that will own the media asset
- `media_type`: `image` | `video`
- `mime_type`: must be in the server allowed list
- `client_media_id`: enables idempotent reuse
- `label`, `display_order`: optional metadata

Local storage note:
- When `MEDIA_DISK=public`, this endpoint returns `422`. Upload files directly with multipart `assets[]` on the submission endpoints instead.
